pattern: A stable three-way split -- collect the smaller, equal, and larger elements into their own order-preserving buckets -- assembles the required arrangement in a single pass.
hints: Instead of swapping elements around in place, imagine sorting each element into one of three buckets: less than the pivot, equal to the pivot, greater than the pivot.
As long as you append to each bucket in the order you encounter the elements, the relative order within each bucket is automatically preserved.
The final answer is just the three buckets placed back to back: everything smaller, then everything equal, then everything larger -- no further reordering needed.

edge_inputs.description: pivot equals the smallest possible value in the array
pivot equals the largest possible value in the array
pivot value repeated several times among smaller and larger elements
every element equal to the pivot, so both other buckets are empty
all elements already less than the pivot except the pivot occurrence itself
all elements already greater than the pivot except the pivot occurrence itself
negative and positive values straddling a zero pivot

stress.description: large random array with the pivot value injected at a random position so it is guaranteed present
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    pivot = rng.randint(-1000000, 1000000)
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    pos = rng.randrange(n)
    nums[pos] = pivot
    return [nums, pivot]
